prose:
Method–provenance block
Property: Atom-condensed Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptor for formaldehyde at its fixed input geometry.
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP) with density fitting (PySCF default).
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz); single-point calculations on this fixed geometry (no re-optimization).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent; continuum models off).
Charge and multiplicity: 0, 1 (neutral closed-shell singlet). Charged N±1 states: cation +1/ doublet; anion −1/ doublet.
Software + version: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (PySCF backend 2.x, ASE optimizer infrastructure not invoked).
Convergence + caveats: SCF cycles for N, N+1, and N–1 states all reported converged by PySCF; Mulliken charges used for population analysis. All integrity checks passed with trustworthy = true, but three warnings (shown verbatim below) were issued.

Selected results (Mulliken scheme; atom order O (1), C (2), H (3), H (4)):
∙ f⁺ = [0.46813824, 0.70839130, −0.08826939, −0.08826015]
∙ f⁻ = [0.46742464, 0.11244422, 0.21007563, 0.21005551]
∙ f⁰ = [0.46778144, 0.41041776, 0.060903121, 0.060897683]
∙ Dual descriptor Δf = [0.00071359555, 0.59594709, −0.29834503, −0.29831566]
Most electrophilic site: C (atom 2) with f⁺ = 0.70839130 (reported above as headline value).
Most nucleophilic site: O (atom 1) with f⁻ = 0.46742464.

Run artifacts
• Live engine log was streamed to the caller’s working directory during execution (file `fukui_YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.out`; view with `tail -f` for SCF details).
• Full result JSON: /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmprfs7tjxh/live.json (contains charges, fukui arrays, integrity checks, and the plotted reactivity map image).

Warnings (verbatim, none omitted):

- sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
- common header present

The calculation was therefore successful, with no convergence problems reported; the warnings merely confirm charge conservation and presence of standard metadata.
